evals.name: review-auth-middleware
evals.prompt: I just pushed some auth middleware changes and want a review before merging. The code is in evals/fixtures/auth-middleware.ts — can you review it for bugs? Follow the bug-review workflow (parallel passes, voting, validation). Treat the file as a PR diff and review it end-to-end.
evals.expected_output: Structured review with findings using the multi-pass voting workflow, categories, trigger scenarios, and severity ratings
assertions: Findings contain at least 3 CRITICAL or HIGH severity issues
Every finding has a non-empty triggerScenario field
Findings include vote counts showing multi-pass consensus
Transcript documents running 3 distinct review passes with voting aggregation

evals.name: review-order-api
evals.prompt: hey can you bug-review my order API changes? File is evals/fixtures/api-orders.ts — it's an Express REST API for orders. Do the full bug-review workflow please.
evals.expected_output: Structured review using parallel passes, voting, and validation with findings in the standard format
assertions: At least one finding identifies an injection vulnerability
Each finding has a valid severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Transcript documents vote counts and aggregation from multiple passes

evals.name: review-clean-utils
evals.prompt: Review evals/fixtures/clean-utils.ts for bugs using the bug-review process. Utility functions — formatCurrency, safeJsonParse, debounce, chunk, retry. Give me a thorough review.
evals.expected_output: Finds only legitimate edge-case bugs with concrete trigger scenarios, or reports no bugs. No false positives.
assertions: Every finding has a concrete reproducible trigger scenario
No finding alleges a security vulnerability
Transcript documents running multiple review passes
